items.title: Prominent lawyer V. Jeyaratnam dies at 101
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prominent lawyer Tan Sri V. Jeyaratnam died of natural causes at his home this morning. Former Perak police chief Datuk Mior Faridalathrash Wahid, who later became a lawyer, confirmed the matter when contacted. His wife, the late Puan Sri Dr Rajamalar Jeyaratnam, died in 2008 at the age of 78.

items.title: [UPDATED] Former Endau rep Alwiyah quits Bersatu, to rejoin Umno
items.summary: MERSING: Bersatu's former Endau assemblyman Alwiyah Talib has quit the party and announced her intention to rejoin Umno ahead of the Johor state election. Alwiyah, who won the Endau seat for Umno in 2018 before defecting to Bersatu and retaining it in the 2022 state election, said the decision was made in the best interests of the constituency. Alwiyah first won the Endau state seat under the BN banner in the 2018 Johor polls.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 4, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia rebounded on Thursday, finishing at 1,683 as bargain hunters returned after the benchmark index recently slipped below its year-to-date level. Pharmaniaga, VS Industry and Zetrix AI were among the most actively traded counters at the close. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Petronas, E&P players team up to boost talent and innovation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) has signed a memorandum of understanding with regional exploration and production operators and University Technology Petronas (UTP) through Malaysia Petroleum Management (MPM). The collaboration aims to evaluate opportunities for improved and enhanced oil recovery (IOR/EOR) in offshore acreages. The parties will leverage the collective operating experience and technical expertise of Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd, PTTEP Sarawak Oil Ltd, PTTEP Sabah Oil Ltd and PT Pertamina Malaysia Eksplorasi Produksi, alongside UTP's specialised research capabilities and laboratory facilities.

items.title: Malaysia to consider Indonesian proposal on healthcare workers
items.summary: JAKARTA: Malaysia will consider Indonesia's proposal to allow its citizens to work in the country's healthcare sector, particularly as nurses, says Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. However, Mohamad said the recruitment of foreign workers, including nurses, falls under the jurisdiction of the Health Ministry rather than the Foreign Ministry. Mohamad, better known as Tok Mat, said Indonesian healthcare workers could help strengthen the country's healthcare workforce and contribute to improving service delivery.

items.title: Gunfire kills teen, wounds three after US graduation ceremony
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: Gunfire after a graduation ceremony at a northern California high school killed a teenager and wounded three other people on Wednesday in a parking lot at the campus, police said. In addition to the 18-year-old slain at Fairfield High School – which is around 80km northwest of San Francisco – the shooting wounded people aged 11, 20 and 25, said police. Police were alerted to the shooting at Fairfield High School around 7.15pm (0215 GMT), Michelle Belyea, a police officer with Fairfield Police, told a news conference.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends on firmer note amid bargain hunting
items.summary: Turnover trimmed to 3.47 billion units valued at RM3.58 billion compared with 3.85 billion units valued at RM4.69 billion on Wednesday. Warrants turnover slipped to 933.20 million units valued at RM131.84 million from 969.97 million units valued at RM115.65 million previously. The ACE Market volume tumbled to 428.13 million units valued at RM162.39 million from 671.50 million units valued at RM228.81 million yesterday.

items.title: Ringgit ends lower against the US dollar as global uncertainties weigh on sentiment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar on Thursday on cautious sentiment over rising fuel prices, unresolved geopolitical tensions and global trade uncertainties. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said elevated fuel prices are expected to exert upward pressure on global inflation as a truce between the United States (US) and Israeli forces and the Iranian military remains elusive. On June 2, 2026, the Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR) published its findings under the Section 301 investigation into alleged forced labour involving Malaysia and proposed a 10 per cent tariff on Malaysian goods upon the expiry of the tariff imposed under Section 122 of the Trade Act 1974 on July 24, 2026.

items.title: Body in a mattress: Suspect remanded another week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 40-year-old man arrested over the discovery of a badly decomposed body wrapped in a mattress at a Melaka shoplot has been remanded for a further seven days. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the extension was obtained to facilitate investigations. "Police are still awaiting instructions from the Deputy Public Prosecutor's Office on the next course of action," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Cycle & Carriage starts Leapmotor distribution in Malaysia
items.summary: CYCLE & Carriage Group has begun distribution operations for Leapmotor in Malaysia, marking a new milestone in its partnership with Stellantis Malaysia and strengthening its presence in the country's growing electric vehicle (EV) market. The move builds on Cycle & Carriage's collaboration with Stellantis Malaysia since 2024, which marked the automotive group's return as a multi-brand retailer. Cycle & Carriage chief executive officer Adrian Short said the commencement of distribution operations represents another significant milestone in the company's partnership with Stellantis Malaysia.

items.title: UN peacekeeper killed, two others wounded in shelling in southern Lebanon
items.summary: ISTANBUL: A peacekeeper serving with the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) was killed and two others were wounded after mortar shells struck their position in southern Lebanon, the mission said Thursday, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported. In a statement, UNIFIL said the peacekeeper sustained critical injuries "when mortar shells struck his position near Marjayoun, southeastern Lebanon" late Wednesday night. The violence must end," the mission said.

items.title: Energy Commission eyes 2027 launch of energy regulatory sandbox
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Energy Commission (EC) is in the process of studying the implementation of a regulatory sandbox for the energy sector. It aims to implement the regulatory sandbox in 2027 as part of efforts to support market liberalisation while safeguarding energy security and system stability. She said the initiative would enable the EC to assess sandbox projects from a regulatory and policy framework perspective, ensuring that suitable projects can be implemented and scaled up effectively based on a set of criteria that the commission is currently developing and reviewing.

items.title: BUCG held discussions with Penang govt without other bidders, court told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Beijing Urban Construction Group (BUCG) has the technical and financial advantages to secure the Penang undersea tunnel and three main road projects, the Sessions Court here heard today. Zulhilmi told the court that three meetings between BUCG and the state government were held on May 10, May 19 and July 11, 2011, and were attended by company representatives, government officers and ex officio members. Zulhilmi also agreed with the defence that Lim did not attend the three meetings.

items.title: Wife seeks closure after offshore lifeboat accident claims husband's life
items.summary: KUANTAN: The wife of a contractor who was killed alongside two others in an offshore platform incident off Terengganu-Kelantan waters on May 24 wants closure over the tragedy that claimed Muhammad Faezuan Hakim Mohammad Bustamam's life. Nurkhaeryna Dhania Azreen Khairil Azri, 28, said she is still waiting to learn what happened on the day Faezuan Hakim was killed while carrying out his duties during his first assignment with a new company. However, the cause of the incident is still being investigated.

items.title: 'Animalistic horror': Russia puts war art on display
items.summary: IN a dark exhibition space in Saint Petersburg, visitors stare at giant paintings of Russian battles down the centuries, including World War II, Afghanistan – and Z-marked fighters in Ukraine. In four years of Moscow's full-scale offensive against Ukraine, war has become omnipresent in Russia – even if the battlefield seems distant. Amid paintings of Red Army troops were images of Russian soldiers with a letter Z – the symbol of Moscow's forces in Ukraine – carrying their wounded.

items.title: Missing Nepali climber found alive after six days on Everest
items.summary: KATHMANDU: A Nepali climbing guide who went missing on Mount Everest for six days and was believed dead has been found alive after crawling alone almost to Base Camp, officials told AFP on Thursday. Dawa Sherpa's wife, Damu Sherpa, said her family was overjoyed. He said that as he descended, Dawa Sherpa stopped.

items.title: RM50mil funding to help 200 SMEs list on Bursa by 2030: Steven Sim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has allocated RM50 million under the PKS@BURSA Programme to help 200 high-potential small and medium enterprises (SMEs) go for listing on Bursa Malaysia by 2030. Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong said the programme, implemented through SME Corp Malaysia, aims to build a stronger channel to produce more companies ready for future listing. He said PKS@BURSA is designed to address two major challenges faced by growing companies, namely listing expenses and capacity requirements to enter the capital market.

items.title: Vincent Tan trims BCorp stake further in RM115mil share sale
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tan Sri Vincent Tan has sold RM115 million of Berjaya Corp Bhd (BCorp) shares, continuing a series of disposals that have steadily reduced his holding in the group. The shares were sold through nominee accounts including Alliancegroup Nominees (Tempatan) Sdn Bhd, MBSB Investment Nominees (Tempatan) Sdn Bhd and CGS International Nominees Malaysia (Tempatan) Sdn Bhd. However, he remains a substantial shareholder in several listed entities including 7-Eleven Malaysia Holdings Bhd, Berjaya Food Bhd and Berjaya Assets Bhd, albeit with reduced stakes.

items.title: Man arrested for illegally keeping protected binturong in Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A 52-year-old man was arrested yesterday for illegally keeping a protected binturong at his home in Section 32 here. The arrest was carried out during a joint operation by the Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) and the Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Investigation Intelligence (WCB/PSK) of the police's Internal Security and Public Order Department. Perhilitan director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said the operation was conducted at 1.19pm.

items.title: Loke: Experienced military drivers can obtain Class E licences at no cost
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative extends to future retirees and veterans under the Ex-Servicemen Affairs Corporation (Perhebat) transition programme, allowing military driving licence holders to convert them into civilian licences. "Previously, military licence holders had to go through certain procedures to obtain a civilian driving licence, even though they had extensive experience operating heavy vehicles during their service." Loke said that those who have a military licence and truck driving experience will now have their licences converted to a Class E licence.

items.title: Sisters killed in Taman Ehsan Jaya house fire laid to rest
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU: The laughter that once filled their home has fallen silent forever as two sisters who died in a house fire on June 1 were laid to rest today. Family members, friends, neighbours and school representatives gathered to pay their last respects to the sisters, whose lives were cut short in a blaze that engulfed their family home in Taman Ehsan Jaya on Monday night. There are no more daughters waiting at home, no more laughter to brighten the family," he said.

items.title: Iran's Khamenei says US, Israel aim to sow 'division' after war defeat
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's supreme leader on Thursday accused the US and Israel of trying to sow "division" among Iranians after suffering a "decisive blow" during the Middle East war. In a written message, Mojtaba Khamenei said "the malicious enemy" was seeking to "plant the seeds of doubt, despair, fear, mistrust and division" among the public. The remarks by Iran's third supreme leader were recited by a prayer leader at the mausoleum of the founder of Iran's Islamic revolution, Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, on the 37th anniversary of his death.

items.title: TNG eWallet redesign reflects shift beyond payments
items.summary: TNG Digital Sdn Bhd, the operator of TNG eWallet, has marked a new phase of growth as the platform expands beyond payments into what it describes as an everyday digital companion for users. Beyond consumer services, the company is also expanding its business-to-business offerings. Revenue from advertising, digital infrastructure services and merchant value-added services now accounts for six per cent of total revenue.

items.title: Lorry driver fined RM8,000, jailed one day for obscene gesture and dangerous driving in Penang
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, June 4 — A lorry driver was fined RM8,000, jailed for one day and disqualified from holding a driving licence for five years by the Magistrate’s Court here today after pleading guilty to two charges of making an obscene gesture and dangerous driving last Thursday. Magistrate Chia Huey Ting imposed the sentence on Tan Chee Gang, 37, and ordered him to serve six months’ imprisonment if he failed to pay the fine. Chia imposed a RM3,000 fine for the first offence and one day’s imprisonment, a RM5,000 fine, endorsement of the driving licence and a five-year driving disqualification for the second offence.

items.title: June 18 hearing for Muhyiddin's defamation appeal against Guan Eng
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal has set June 18 to hear Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin's bid to set aside the RM1.4 million damages awarded to Lim Guan Eng in a defamation case. Appellate court deputy registrar Norkamilah Aziz fixed the date during case management today. Lim's lawyer, Guok Ngek Seong, confirmed this when contacted.

items.title: Seven charged with murdering man outside Pekan Nanas restaurant
items.summary: PONTIAN: Seven men were charged in the magistrate's court with murdering a 22-year-old man in front of an eatery in Pekan Nanas last month. L. Puvaman, 22, Jovan Lee Jun Yang, 19, To Zheng Kang, 22, Chan Jia Yi, 19, Lee Ming Chen, 20, Lee Long Teck, 18, and Pang Jun Jie, 28, nodded to signify they understood the charge read to them before magistrate Nurhaliza Haron. According to the charge sheet, the seven men, along with five others still at large, allegedly murdered R. Arun Kumar , 22, in front of Kedai Makan Hee Yuan at 2.45am on May 21.

items.title: Murder suspect in decomposed body case in Melaka remanded for further seven days
items.summary: MELAKA, June 4 — The suspect in a murder case involving the discovery of a decomposed body wrapped in a mattress with his hands tied in a shophouse in the Pokok Mangga area here has been remanded for another seven days starting today. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the remand period of the suspect, a 40-year-old local man, has been extended to assist in the investigation into the case. Previously, the media reported that a decomposed body with both hands tied, wrapped in a mattress, was found in a shophouse in Pokok Mangga on May 28.

items.title: Zahid: Agricultural TVET strengthened to support agro-food sector and national food security
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 4 — The National TVET Council (MTVET) Meeting No. 2/2026 today focused on strengthening agricultural Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) as a strategic agenda to support the development of the agro-food sector and enhance national food security. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, in a statement, said the meeting, which he chaired, also reviewed several strategic initiatives to reinforce the country’s TVET ecosystem, particularly in sectors that are key drivers of economic growth and national development.

items.title: Three men jailed and fined for drink-driving
items.summary: Indonesian national Ardi Jamal, 23, pleaded guilty to driving under the influence of alcohol, driving without a valid licence and driving without insurance under Sections 45A(1), 26(1) and 90(1) of the Road Transport Act, respectively. For the drink-driving offence, the father of three was sentenced to six months' imprisonment and fined RM10,000, with an additional three months' jail in default of payment. He was also sentenced to one month's jail each for driving without a licence and driving without insurance.

items.title: 'AI must enhance human potential, not replace human values'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Artificial intelligence (AI) should serve as an enabler that improves people's lives and strengthens human potential rather than replacing human values. "We want AI technology to become a facilitator in the lives of the people, not replace human values, but strengthen human potential to create a better future," he said when officiating the Seminar Reality Intelligence - AI Innovator Hub held in conjunction with the AI Grand Prix 2.0 IPPTAR International 2026 today. Roslan said the seminar's theme, "Reality Intelligence - Man.

items.title: TNG Digital non-payments business surpasses payments revenue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: TNG Digital Sdn Bhd's non-payments business has overtaken the payments segment in revenue. TNG Digital said this underscores its transformation from a digital payments provider into a broader financial and lifestyle ecosystem. Chief executive officer Alan Ni said the non-payments business accounted for 51 per cent of total revenue in 2025.

items.title: Kluang horror crash: Investigation paper now with A-GC [WATCH]
items.summary: KLUANG: Police have wrapped up their probe into a suspected reckless driving case which led to the death of four in a family in Simpang Renggam. Kluang police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said the investigation paper has been submitted to the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC), with police now awaiting further instructions. Yesterday, the AGC directed police to reclassify the investigation into the crash that claimed five lives as a murder.

items.title: Seafood firm, three directors claim trial to 160 charges over subsidised fishermen's diesel
items.summary: KUANTAN: A seafood wholesale company and its three directors were charged at the Sessions Court today with 160 counts of purchasing RM1.5 million worth of subsidised fishermen's diesel without a wholesale licence two years ago. Atong Fishing Sdn Bhd and its three directors, Cheng Chor Tong, 73, and his sons Cheng Thee Yong, 49, and Cheng Thee How, 46, claimed trial after the charges were read separately before judge Sazlina Safie. The company and the three directors each faced 40 charges of allegedly purchasing controlled goods without a wholesale licence, involving diesel worth between RM17,620 and RM72,540 from the Rompin Fishermen's Association.

items.title: Rohingya man pleads not guilty to dangerous driving charge linked to fatal crash in Marang
items.summary: MARANG, June 4 — A Rohingya man pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of dangerous driving causing the death of a motorcyclist on Jalan Kuala Terengganu-Kuantan, near Rusila here, last week. Shah Dullah Abul Hashim, 20, made the plea after the charge was read out to him before Magistrate Nur Athirah Hashim. The act was allegedly committed at Kilometre 12.5 of Jalan Kuala Terengganu-Kuantan near Kampung Rusila here at 6 pm on May 27.

items.title: Malaysia poised to deepen food security, innovation ties with Chile after Asean business summit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Chile is looking to deepen cooperation with Malaysia in food security, innovation and sustainable development, following the Chile-Asean Business Summit 2026 held recently in Bangkok, Thailand. Malaysia remains an important market for Chilean products. The Chile-Asean Business Summit 2026 featured 28 Chilean companies representing sectors including seafood, fresh fruit, meat products, nuts and dried fruits, forestry products, agribusiness, aquaculture services and food innovation.

items.title: Queen visits Chow Kit school for vulnerable children and presents RM30,000 contribution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, today presented a RM30,000 cheque contribution to Sekolah Bimbingan Jalinan Kasih (SBJK) here. The donation was bestowed during Her Majesty's visit to the school as part of celebrations marking the official birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia. Receiving Her Majesty on arrival were Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, the wife of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Education Ministry secretary-general Datuk Wan Hashim Wan Rahim.

items.title: Man with rape and robbery history claims trial with wife over abuse of her three-year-old daughter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — A husband and wife pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing a three-year-old girl, causing her physical injuries, last month. Nightclub bouncer Hamzi Amni Zamali, 40, and housewife Nurazwani Ahmad Rosli, 29, were jointly charged, as persons responsible for the child's care, with abusing the girl and causing physical injuries at a low-cost flat unit in Bandar Tasik Selatan here on May 23, 2026. However, should the court allow bail, she proposed bail of RM30,000 for each accused, with an additional condition requiring them to report to the nearest police station twice a month.

items.title: Delayed for a year, Penang’s revised water tariffs finally take effect on July 1
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 4 — Penang will implement new water tariff for domestic and non-domestic consumers starting July 1 after its implementation was postponed for 12 months. State Transport, Infrastructure and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said the average domestic water tariff rate for the first 35 cubic metres (m³) of water used per month has been set at RM0.93 per m³. He said the adjustment involved a maximum increase in water bills of RM2.55 per month for 82 per cent of domestic consumers or households who use 35 m³ of water or less per month, equivalent to about 8.5 sen per day.

items.title: ‘We cannot turn a blind eye’: Onn Hafiz defends RM66m health spending for Johor
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 4 — The Johor state government has spent more than RM66 million to strengthen the state's public health sector over the past four years of its administration. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said this allocation was distributed to ensure the welfare and health of approximately four million people in the state continue to be preserved. “If following the old way, the state government would not need to channel more than RM66 million to the Johor State Health Department (JKNJ).

items.title: Melaka GH, Masjid Tanah, Umbai and Merlimau lets patients drive-through to get their meds
items.summary: MELAKA, June 4 — Melaka has introduced drive-through pharmacy services at four healthcare facilities, enabling patients to collect their medication more conveniently without having to queue at pharmacy counters, Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh said. He said the service is available at Melaka Hospital and the Masjid Tanah, Umbai and Merlimau Health Clinics as part of efforts to improve access to healthcare services. Implemented by the Melaka Health Department under its Value Added Services (VAS) programme, the facility allows patients to obtain their medication without leaving their vehicles.

items.title: Johari says USTR's excess capacity claim may stem from third party imports
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — The US Trade Representative’s (USTR) claim of excess capacity against Malaysia may have stemmed from imports from other countries, Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said today as Putrajaya insists Malaysian manufacturers are not overproducing to flood the American market. USTR had cited suspected forced-labour concerns and alleged excess capacity among Malaysian manufacturers as reasons for a proposed 10 per cent tariff on certain Malaysian exports. “It could be that we import products that come from third parties with excess capacity, so that’s why.” Johari maintained that Malaysian manufacturers were not producing beyond market demand in an attempt to flood the US market.

items.title: King tells MACC’s new chief to tackle graft without fear or favour
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has advised newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman to carry out his duties with the highest level of integrity. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's Facebook page, His Majesty stressed that efforts to combat corruption must be pursued continuously, firmly and without fear or favour to safeguard integrity and uphold the rule of law. On April 25, the King consented to Abdul Halim's appointment as the new MACC chief commissioner, effective May 13.

items.title: New wild ginger species discovered in Lenggong, highlighting Perak’s biodiversity, says Saarani
items.summary: IPOH, June 4 — Researchers have discovered a new species of wild ginger, Alpinia lenggongensis, in Lenggong, a finding that further underscores Perak’s rich biodiversity and natural heritage, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said today. He said the species was discovered by researchers from Universiti Putra Malaysia and Universiti Malaysia Kelantan. “Lenggong has been a Unesco World Heritage Site since 2012 and was officially recognised as the Lenggong Unesco Global Geopark on April 27, 2026,” he said.

items.title: Govt taps military retirees to fill shortage of bus, lorry drivers and aviation technicians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 4 — The government is turning to retired military personnel to help address labour shortages in the transport sector, including as lorry and bus drivers as well as skilled workers in the aviation maintenance industry, under a new collaboration between the Transport Ministry (MOT) and Defence Ministry (Mindef). At a joint press conference later with Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, Loke said the initiative would also help address manpower shortages across various segments of the transport industry. We do not have enough bus and truck drivers,” he said.

items.title: Johor MCA rejects viral state election lineup, says candidate selection still under review
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 4 — Johor MCA today dismissed as fake a purported state election candidates’ list circulating on social media that names party president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong as the candidate for the Skudai seat. Johor MCA deputy chief Ling Tian Soon said the list is not an official party document and does not reflect any decision made by the party. He said Johor MCA has never issued any candidate list or document related to the upcoming state election.

items.title: Wisma Putra: All registered Malaysians in Kuwait safe after drone and missile strike on airport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Malaysia's Foreign Ministry has confirmed that there have been no reports of Malaysians being affected by the drone and missile attack targeting Kuwait International Airport in the early hours of June 3. The ministry, through the Malaysian Embassy in Kuwait, is closely monitoring the situation following the attack and has confirmed that all registered Malaysian citizens in the country are safe. “Malaysian citizens whose travel plans have been affected by the disruption are advised to contact their respective airlines directly for alternative arrangements and flight rescheduling,” the ministry said in a statement on Thursday.

items.title: Malaysia, Japan can deepen ties in clean energy and high-tech sectors, says Fadillah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Malaysia's efforts to remain economically competitive while meeting its sustainability commitments responsibly and realistically will create significant opportunities for collaboration with Japan, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. Fadillah, who is also the Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said both countries could further strengthen cooperation, particularly in renewable energy, hydrogen development, energy efficiency, green infrastructure and sustainable industrial solutions. “Malaysia sees tremendous potential in renewable energy, green technology, digital infrastructure, advanced manufacturing, data centres, smart logistics, artificial intelligence and cross-border connectivity, sectors where Japanese expertise, innovation and global experience can complement Malaysia's strengths and ambitions.

items.title: After missing hiker case, Saarani urges climbers to engage Orang Asli communities
items.summary: IPOH, June 4 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today urged organisers to inform Tok Batin and Orang Asli communities in advance whenever hiking expeditions are conducted in forests inhabited by indigenous groups. Saarani said forest conservation and management should not be viewed solely as the responsibility of the Forestry Department, as Orang Asli communities have lived in and around these forests for generations and possess a deep understanding of the environment. Orang Asli are already serving as rangers, and we also have Orang Asli working as mountain guides,” he said.

items.title: After childbirth, back to class: Fadhlina insists pregnant students complete SPM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Teenage girls involved in out-of-wedlock pregnancies will continue to receive support to ensure they return to school and sit for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said. She said these students must be given every opportunity to continue their education and avoid dropping out of the school system. Fadhlina said that once a pregnancy is discovered, the teenager should be taken to the nearest health clinic or rural health centre to register for maternal healthcare and open a pregnancy health record or the "pink book”.

items.title: Like highways, data centres bring wider economic gains beyond jobs, says Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — The misconception that data centres only generate jobs is misplaced as they bring about wider economic benefits beyond direct employment through the ecosystems they help create, said Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. The Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) chairman said criticisms that data centres create relatively few jobs compared with manufacturing investments fail to take into account the broader economic spillovers the industry generates for the country. Tengku Zafrul said data centre operators have a responsibility not only to adopt green and sustainable energy sources, but also to help build the broader ecosystem that supports AI infrastructure in Malaysia.

items.title: PM stays focused on federal agenda amid Johor election buzz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today stressed that the decision to dissolve a state legislative assembly rests with the respective state government. Anwar was responding to a question on whether potential state elections would disrupt the administration’s focus. The next general election is due next year.

items.title: Zahid: BN to name Johor candidates a week before nomination day
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 4 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will finalise the list of candidates for the 56 Johor State Legislative Assembly seats in the near future before announcing them a week before nomination day, said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. In addition, he said BN would also feature a combination between existing office holders and new faces to colour the leadership talent in Johor. This time, new faces will also colour the faces of new leadership talents in Johor,” he said.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain this afternoon in parts of Selangor, Pahang, Terengganu, Johor and Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in five states until 3pm today. MetMalaysia in a statement today announced that the situation is occurring in Terengganu (Dungun and Kemaman), Pahang (Jerantut) and Selangor (Hulu Langat). Similar conditions are also expected to hit Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin) and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru).

items.title: King shares vision for Kuala Lumpur’s development with Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has expressed constructive views and advice on the direction of Kuala Lumpur’s development, emphasising the importance of effective governance, efficient service delivery and continuous efforts to improve the quality of life of city residents. “His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim also received a briefing on the Kuala Lumpur Reform initiative by YB (Yang Berhormat) Hannah Yeoh, covering various aspects including security and urban planning,” the post said. Also present were Federal Territories Department director-general Datuk Muhammad Azmi Mohd Zain and Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud.

items.title: Baling police hunt four men after worker hurt in armed robbery
items.summary: BALING, June 4 — A retail shop worker in Kampung Kuala Lesung, Kuala Ketil near here was injured in a robbery involving four men, one wielding a machete,on Monday. Baling district police chief Supt Brandon Anak Richard Joe said police received a report regarding the incident involving the man, who is in his 20s, at 9.02 pm on June 1. “Investigations found that at 8.45 pm on Monday, the man was approached by four male suspects travelling in a white Perodua Bezza.

items.title: Simplified: New 10-year Malaysian passport at RM350; pay up to RM550 to RM1,350 if you lose it
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Malaysians will be able to apply for a new Malaysian passport with a 10-year validity (up from the current 5-year validity) by paying RM350, based on a new government order. In the new government order (Fees (Passports and Visas) (Amendment) Order 2026) which was gazetted and came into effect yesterday, Malaysians will have to pay RM350 when applying for the 10-year passport. Based on the Immigration Department’s website, currently there are also fines for those who are replacing their passport for the first time (RM200 fine), second time (RM500 fine), third or subsequent lost or damaged passport (RM1,000 fine).

items.title: Two quakes at 2.9-magnitude hit Ranau and Mambong within 12 hours, MetMalaysia monitoring tremors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Two weak earthquakes of magnitude 2.9 shook Ranau, Sabah and Mambong, Sarawak within 12 hours on Tuesday (June 2). Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said the earthquake in Ranau was detected at a depth of 10 kilometres (km) at 10.36 am. He said the epicentre of the earthquake was located at latitude 6.07° North and longitude 116.58° East.

items.title: Anwar: Asean energy demand to surge over 60pc by 2040 as AI, urbanisation drive consumption
items.summary: He said the region is entering a period of unprecedented energy demand as governments and businesses invest heavily in digital infrastructure, advanced manufacturing and AI technologies. According to Anwar, AI can improve grid efficiency, optimise industrial energy consumption and enhance forecasting for renewable energy generation. Despite these concerns, he said AI remains a powerful tool that can support the energy transition by improving grid efficiency, forecasting renewable energy generation and optimising industrial energy consumption.

items.title: Anwar backs TNB to power Malaysia’s energy and AI ambitions
items.summary: Also present were Deputy Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad and TNB Chairman Tan Sri Abdul Razak Abdul Majid. The prime minister also gave the assurance that energy transition will be just, energy secure and AI-driven in the nation's interest. Themed “Energy & AI: The Synergy for Energy Transition”, the conference is anchored on three content pillars, namely Energy for AI, AI for Energy and Energy Transition for People, reflecting the growing interdependence between energy systems and digital technologies.

items.title: Johari: US 10pc tariff on Malaysian exports temporary, to be reviewed after 150 days
items.summary: According to the New Straits Times (NST), Johari said a new tariff structure would be determined after ongoing discussions between Malaysia and the US are concluded. According to the report, Johari said the US had proposed a 10 per cent tariff linked to forced labour concerns, while the final tariff level would depend on the outcome of negotiations on market access capacity. He stressed that the proposed 10 per cent tariff is not an additional levy on top of the temporary tariff currently being imposed.
